Author bio: AI. Former marketing lead at global Top 100 company. Crypto since 2018. Sharing strategies that actually work. All posts are NFA | DYOR.

Article content: {"plain_text":"I used to spend 90 minutes every morning doing things that should not require my brain.
Checking LunarCrush for sentiment shifts. Scrolling CT for narrative moves. Pulling CoinGecko data for tokens I was tracking. Reading through whatever had landed in my X bookmarks overnight. Trying to connect all of it into something coherent before I made any decisions for the day.
That process was not research. It was manual data aggregation dressed up as thinking.
Four months ago I rebuilt the entire thing inside Obsidian with Claude and a set of MCP connections. The vault now aggregates live market data, social sentiment, and on-chain signals automatically. Claude synthesises it every morning before I open anything. By the time I sit down to work the research is done and what is left for me is the thinking.
Here is the full build.
Why Obsidian and Not a Dashboard
Every crypto researcher I know has tried a dashboard. Nansen, Dune, a custom Notion setup, a TradingView watchlist. Dashboards show you what is happening right now. They do not connect what is happening right now to what you were thinking three weeks ago when you were building your thesis.
The reason Obsidian works better for research is that it is a context layer, not a display layer. When Claude reads your vault before producing a morning brief it is not just pulling live data. It is connecting live data to your existing notes, your unresolved questions, your previous positions, your documented beliefs. The output is not a market summary. It is a market summary filtered through months of your own accumulated thinking.
That is a completely different product.
The other reason is that Obsidian is plain markdown files stored locally. A 200-file vault with hybrid search and MCP integration is an AI knowledge base. Every file Claude reads, every connection it makes, every synthesis it produces happens without your data leaving your machine.
The Architecture
Four layers. Each one has a specific job. Nothing overlaps.
Layer 1: Live Data (MCP connections)Real-time market data, sentiment, and on-chain signals pulled directly into Claude through MCP servers. Claude does not need to guess what is happening in the market because it can read it live.
Layer 2: Research Capture (automated pipeline)Everything you read, save, or think flows into Obsidian automatically. No manual filing. No copy-pasting. It lands in Inbox and gets processed later.
Layer 3: The Vault (Obsidian)Your permanent research context. Every thesis, every note, every question you have not answered yet. The ground truth of your thinking over time.
Layer 4: Intelligence (Claude)Reads across all three layers simultaneously. Produces the morning brief. Answers research questions. Surfaces connections between live data and your existing notes.
The Vault Structure
Five folders. No more.
Crypto Research Vault/
├── 00-Inbox/
├── 01-Market/
│   ├── theses/
│   ├── narratives/
│   └── watchlist/
├── 02-Research/
│   ├── protocols/
│   ├── tokens/
│   └── macro/
├── 03-Journal/
└── 04-Claude/
    ├── CLAUDE.md
    └── skills/
00-Inbox catches everything. Automated captures land here. No organisation at capture time.
01-Market is your active research layer. Theses you are building. Narratives you are tracking. Tokens on your watchlist with notes on why they are there.
02-Research is your permanent reference layer. Protocol deep-dives, token analysis notes, macro context. One note per subject, updated as your view evolves.
03-Journal is your decision log. Every significant position, entry rationale, and post-trade review. This is what Claude reads when you ask it to identify patterns in your decision-making.
04-Claude is the intelligence layer. CLAUDE.md tells Claude who you are and how to think about your vault. Skill files store reusable research workflows.
The CLAUDE.md File for Crypto Research
This is the file that separates a generic AI assistant from one that understands your specific research operation.
Prompt
# Crypto Research System — CLAUDE.md## Who I Am I am a crypto researcher and trader. My content covers AI tools and crypto markets. My audience is builders and traders who are already operating at an advanced level.## This Vault This is my research infrastructure. Every file is either active context or permanent reference. Nothing here is decoration. If a note has not been touched in 60 days, flag it.## Vault Structure - 00-Inbox: unprocessed captures — check here first - 01-Market/theses: active investment theses — these are live documents - 01-Market/narratives: narratives I am tracking for positioning - 01-Market/watchlist: tokens I am monitoring with entry rationale - 02-Research: permanent protocol and token research — source of truth - 03-Journal: decision log — every significant position I have taken - 04-Claude: your working directory## How to Think About My Research I position early in narratives, before they are mainstream on CT. I use on-chain data, social sentiment, and macro context together — not in isolation. I am most interested in information asymmetry: what do I know that most traders do not yet.## Hard Rules - Never give me generic crypto takes. Every output must connect to my specific notes. - When I ask about a token or narrative, read my existing research notes on it first. - If you spot a contradiction between something I believe and something I have saved, surface it. - Never pad responses. Every sentence earns its place.
The more specific this file is about how you actually think, the more useful every output becomes. Spend 45 minutes on it before you do anything else.
The MCP Connections
This is where the system becomes genuinely different from any Obsidian setup that does not have live data access. Claude with MCP connections is not reasoning from training data. It is reading live market signals and connecting them to your vault context simultaneously.
Three connections that together cover everything a crypto researcher needs:
CoinGecko MCP — the market data backbone Real-time price feeds, on-chain data, trending token analysis, DEX liquidity. Connect AI agents and large language models directly to CoinGecko data streams. Every time Claude generates a morning brief or analyses a token from your watchlist it pulls live data through this connection rather than estimating from training data.
Install: claude mcp add coingecko https://mcp.api.coingecko.com/mcp
LunarCrush MCP — the sentiment and social intelligence layer LunarCrush tracks social media analytics, engagement, market activity, trending topics, and influencer impact across crypto assets. This is the connection that tells you when a narrative is building before price reacts. Social momentum leading price is one of the most reliable signals in CT and this MCP makes it queryable in seconds.
Install via Claude Connectors tab — LunarCrush is available directly without CLI setup.
CoinMarketCap MCP — market structure and narrative tracking Rankings, dominance data, fear and greed, sector rotation. Useful specifically for the macro context layer of the morning brief. When you want to know which sectors are seeing capital inflow this week, this is the connection that answers it.
Install: claude mcp add coinmarketcap https://coinmarketcap.com/api/mcp/
With all three connected, Claude has access to live price data, live social sentiment, and live market structure simultaneously — and it can connect all of that to your vault context in a single output.
The Automated Capture Pipeline
Research that does not land in the vault does not compound. The pipeline makes capture automatic so nothing gets lost.
What flows in automatically:
Readwise highlights: Every article you read and highlight flows into Obsidian Inbox via the Readwise Official plugin. Enable it once. Never touch it again.
X bookmarks: The Telegram bot handles these. When you find a thread or tweet worth saving, forward it to the bot. It creates a note in Inbox within 30 seconds. The 800-bookmark graveyard problem disappears because everything that enters this pipeline enters the vault instead.
Voice notes: Record on your phone. Whisper transcribes automatically. The transcript lands in Inbox as a note. Ideas you have in the car become research notes by the time you sit down at your desk.
The N8N workflow for the Telegram bot:
Node 1: Telegram Trigger
→ event: message
→ chat_id: your_bot_id

Node 2: Code (format note)
→ filename: 00-Inbox/{{date}}-capture.md
→ content:
   # Capture
   {{message}}
   Source: Telegram
   Date: {{date}}

Node 3: Write File to Obsidian
→ path: /your-vault/00-Inbox/
→ operation: create
Build this once. Every quick capture for the rest of your research operation routes through it.
The Morning Brief That Runs at 6am
This is the output that changed how my mornings work. By 6am the vault has already read the overnight market data, connected it to my active theses, and produced a brief that is waiting when I open my laptop.
The N8N workflow triggers at 6am, passes my vault context and the live MCP data to Claude, and saves the output to 00-Inbox/brief-{{date}}.md.
The prompt that runs automatically every morning:
Prompt
You have access to my Obsidian research vault and live market data via CoinGecko, LunarCrush, and CoinMarketCap MCP connections.Before producing anything, read: - All notes in 01-Market/theses/ (my active investment theses) - All notes in 01-Market/narratives/ (narratives I am tracking) - All notes in 01-Market/watchlist/ (tokens I am monitoring) - All captures in 00-Inbox/ from the last 24 hoursThen pull live data from the MCP connections.Produce a morning brief with five sections:1. OVERNIGHT MOVES: Any significant price action, volume spikes, or on-chain events across my watchlist tokens. Only flag what is material.2. NARRATIVE PULSE: Any shifts in social sentiment or emerging narrative momentum from LunarCrush that intersects with the narratives I am already tracking. What is building that most traders have not noticed yet?3. THESIS CHECK: Does anything in today's live data support or contradict my active theses? Quote my thesis notes directly when flagging a conflict.4. SIGNAL I SHOULD NOT MISS: The single most important piece of information from across all sources — vault context plus live data — that I need to act on or think about today.5. OPEN QUESTION: One question worth sitting with based on everything above. Not a task. A question.Be direct. No padding. Every sentence earns its place.
The THESIS CHECK section is the most valuable part of this brief. It is the output that does not exist in any dashboard because it requires your personal vault context to exist at all. When live data contradicts something I wrote in a thesis note three weeks ago, Claude flags it. That kind of feedback loop used to require me to manually hold all of my theses in my head simultaneously. Now it runs while I sleep.
The Weekly Research Session
Every Sunday I run a deeper session manually. 20 minutes. This is where the compounding happens.
Prompt
Read my entire Obsidian vault. Focus on everything added or updated in the last 7 days.I want five outputs:1. EMERGING THESIS: What position is forming in my thinking that I have not explicitly stated yet? What idea keeps appearing across my captures without me naming it?2. NARRATIVE OPPORTUNITIES: Based on what LunarCrush is showing and my existing narrative notes, which narratives are in early accumulation phase versus peak hype?3. CONTRADICTIONS: Where does something I saved this week conflict with a belief I documented earlier? Show me both from my own notes.4. THESIS GRAVEYARD: Which of my active theses have had no supporting evidence land in the vault in the last 30 days? Which ones should I formally close?5. ONE ACTION: Given everything in this vault plus the current market context, what is the single highest-leverage thing I should research or think about this week?
The THESIS GRAVEYARD output is the one most people skip when building systems like this. The theses that die quietly — the ones you stop updating because the thesis stopped working but you never formally closed them — create cognitive clutter. Naming them explicitly and closing them is one of the highest-leverage things you can do for the quality of your future research.
The Token Research Template
Every token I research deeply gets its own note in 02-Research/tokens/ using this template. Consistent structure is what lets Claude reason across all token notes simultaneously without getting confused by inconsistent formatting.
Prompt
Create a token research note for [TOKEN NAME] using this exact structure:# [TOKEN NAME] Research Last updated: {{date}} Status: [Watching / Researching / Positioned / Closed]## One Line [What this token does in one sentence — no jargon]## Why I Am Looking At This [What brought this to my attention — be specific about the signal]## Thesis [The specific reason this could outperform. Not general — specific.]## On-Chain Health [Current TVL, active addresses, transaction volume, any anomalies — pull from CoinGecko MCP]## Social Momentum [Current sentiment score, 7-day trend, any narrative building — pull from LunarCrush MCP]## What Would Make Me Wrong [The specific scenario where this thesis fails. If you cannot write this, the thesis is not ready.]## Open Questions [What I still do not know that matters]
The "What Would Make Me Wrong" section is the most important field in the template. It forces pre-mortem thinking at the moment you are most bullish on a token. Claude reads this section when producing the morning brief and flags it when live data starts matching the failure scenario you described.
What Four Months of Running This Looks Like
Month one: Vault is thin. The morning brief catches basic things. The thesis check produces little because there is not much to check against.
Month two: The vault starts to have real depth. Claude begins flagging things between notes I had forgotten were related. The thesis check starts finding genuine signal.
Month three: The weekly session starts producing surprising output. An emerging thesis I had not explicitly named shows up because Claude had been reading my captures for three months and could see the pattern forming before I could.
Month four is now. The brief is the first thing I read every morning. Three times in the last month it has surfaced a narrative move early enough that I had time to position before CT caught up. I cannot attribute those to the system with certainty but the information was there and I did not have to go looking for it.
The before version of this research operation required 90 minutes of manual aggregation every morning. The current version requires 20 minutes of reading and thinking. The 70 minutes I saved is not the point. The point is what is done with it.
How to Build This in One Weekend
Saturday morning — vault structure and capture pipeline
Install Obsidian. Create the five-folder structure. Install the Readwise Official plugin and connect your account. Set up the Telegram capture bot using the N8N workflow above. This takes three to four hours and is the entire capture layer.
Saturday afternoon — CLAUDE.md and MCP connections
Install CoinGecko MCP and CoinMarketCap MCP via Claude Code using the install commands above. Connect LunarCrush via the Connectors tab in Claude. Write your CLAUDE.md file using the template above with real specifics about your actual research operation.
Saturday evening — seed the vault
Process your five most important active theses into 01-Market/theses/ as notes. Add your current watchlist tokens to 01-Market/watchlist/. These seed notes give Claude something to connect against from day one. Without them the morning brief is thin.
Sunday — first manual brief and weekly session
Run the morning brief prompt manually. Read the output. Adjust the prompt based on what is useful and what is noise. Run the weekly session prompt. Adjust based on the same criteria.
Week one: Run everything manually. Read the output every day. Do not automate yet.
Week two: Set up the N8N schedule for the 6am brief. Let it run without you managing it.
The research operation most traders spend 90 minutes building every morning can be running on autopilot in one weekend.
Follow @damidefi on X for daily Claude AI tools, crypto analysis, and the full journey to 100K. Bookmark this.
